text: The video demonstrates near-infrared laparoscopic fluorescence highlighting fluorescent areas at the base of the mesocolon, corresponding to lymph nodes along the superior rectal artery during total mesorectal excision for rectal cancer. This visual guidance aids in identifying fascial planes and nodal tissue for medial to lateral dissection along the major arterial axis.

The surgeon uses a proctoscope to inject three milliliters of indocyanine green submucosally near the rectal cancer. The Pinpoint laparoscopic system from Novadact Technologies is employed, providing both high-definition white light and near-infrared imaging to create an augmented reality view visible in the upper middle left part of the screen.

The surgical team switches the main screen image between white light, near-infrared, and a combination view to enhance visualization of lymphatic channels and nodes during the operation. Indocyanine green highlights these structures, which follow embryological fascial planes, without indicating cancer cell presence.

In the procedure for a sixty-two-year-old patient with rectal cancer, the surgeon performs a total mesorectal excision. The near-infrared fluorescence imaging provides clear visualization of lymph nodes, particularly highlighting retroperitoneal lymph nodes. The dissection follows the medial to lateral plane, similar to the previous case.

The surgeon performs posterior and lateral dissection of the mesorectum, utilizing near-infrared fluorescence to visualize and color-code lymphatic channels and lymph nodes. As the mesorectum is mobilized forward, green fluorescent points indicating mesorectal lymph nodes are visible. The dissection requires careful differentiation between the visceral and parietal fascia due to secondary lymphatic uptake within the retroperitoneum.

During the ongoing dissection, lymphatic channels draining the rectum become increasingly visible, with specific fluorescent points indicating nodes within the mesorectum. The near-infrared imaging highlights these structures, aiding in the identification of embryological planes necessary for oncological resection.

This video is itself associated with a dedicated special supplement of the journal Colorectal Disease, which contains much more detail on the system, technique and applications of near-infrared fluorescence laparoscopy in contemporary practice.
